title: Fast risk_check does not evaluate the daily-loss kill intraday (fed zero_ret)
source: Adversarial code review 2026-05-29 (risk-officer + general-purpose reviewers on armed execution layer)
why: The 60s risk_check feeds apply_risk_controls zero_ret, so portfolio_kill/killed (daily-loss) can never fire on the fast path -- only the DD-from-HWM latch is live intraday. The docstring claims it evaluates the daily-loss kill: doc/behavior mismatch.
scope: risk-officer FINDING4: EITHER wire a real same-day return (equity vs start-of-day equity) so the daily-loss kill is live intraday, OR correct the docstring to stop claiming it runs on the fast path. DD-from-HWM is the dominant ruin guard so impact is low, but the doc is misleading.

history.reason: Logged from adversarial review of armed execution layer
@ fix(execution): adversarial review caught 2 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH bugs in armed layer
fix(execution): adversarial review caught 2 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH bugs in armed layer
fix(execution): adversarial review caught 2 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH bugs in armed layer

Wired a real INTRADAY daily-loss kill into the fast risk_check: LiveState now tracks a persisted start-of-day equity baseline (roll_session_equity, survives mid-day restart); risk_check flattens when same-day loss >= portfolio_kill. NON-latched (matches backtest daily-budget semantics; run_cycle resumes next day). Docstring corrected to stop claiming the apply_risk_controls daily kill runs on the fast path. TDD test.
fix(execution): resolve B0093/B0094/B0095 review findings; B0090 -> done
